name: clear_always_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_clear_always_empty(pre: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post: CommitMask)
    requires
        post@ == pre@.difference(other@),
    ensures
        post@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Would mean clear erases everything regardless of other, making it a total wipe instead of a selective clear

name: clear_is_noop
code:
proof fn phi_2_clear_is_noop(pre: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post: CommitMask)
    requires
        post@ == pre@.difference(other@),
    ensures
        post@ == pre@,
{
}
reason: Would mean clear never removes any bits, making the operation completely ineffective

name: other_survives_clear
code:
proof fn phi_3_other_survives_clear(pre: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post: CommitMask)
    requires
        post@ == pre@.difference(other@),
    ensures
        other@.subset_of(post@),
{
}
reason: Would mean the bits we intended to clear are still present in the result, defeating the purpose of clear

name: result_confined_to_other
code:
proof fn phi_4_result_confined_to_other(pre: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post: CommitMask)
    requires
        post@ == pre@.difference(other@),
    ensures
        post@.subset_of(other@),
{
}
reason: Would mean the result only contains bits from other, which is the exact opposite of what set difference should produce

name: clear_symmetric
code:
proof fn phi_5_clear_symmetric(pre: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post: CommitMask)
    requires
        post@ == pre@.difference(other@),
    ensures
        post@ == other@.difference(pre@),
{
}
reason: Would mean set difference is symmetric (A\B == B\A), which is false in general and would indicate a fundamentally broken spec
